skill.name: security-bluebook-builder
skill.description: Build security Blue Books for sensitive applications using structured guidance and review patterns.
skill.summary: Create structured security Blue Books for sensitive application reviews.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged GitHub source URLs and weak-crypto keyword matches in markdown text. Review found no executable code, no network behavior, no cryptographic implementation, and no prompt injection attempt.

low_findings.title: Static false positive: documentation URLs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are GitHub source references in metadata and documentation. They do not perform network requests or send user data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both locations are plain markdown or YAML references to a GitHub repository. No executable network call or data transfer instruction is present.

low_findings.title: Static false positive: weak crypto keyword match
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts match ordinary descriptive text. No DES, MD5, SHA1, or other cryptographic algorithm use appears in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain a description and generic guidance sentence only. There is no cryptographic operation, dependency, or implementation context.

content.user_title: Build Security Bluebooks for Sensitive Apps
content.value_statement: Security reviews often miss context when findings are scattered across notes and tickets. This skill helps structure a clear Blue Book for sensitive applications.
actual_capabilities: Guides creation of security Blue Books for sensitive applications.
Provides high-level patterns for organizing security review material.
Helps define when to use a Blue Book workflow.
Supports documentation of application risks and review context.
Can help turn security notes into a structured review artifact.
limitations: Does not include scripts, scanners, or automated testing tools.
Does not validate live systems or source code by itself.
Requires user-provided application context and security evidence.
Provides guidance, not legal, compliance, or certification approval.

use_cases.title: Prepare an App Security Review
use_cases.description: Create a Blue Book outline before reviewing a sensitive internal application.

use_cases.title: Document Launch Risk
use_cases.description: Summarize controls, open risks, and review decisions for a product launch.

use_cases.title: Standardize Security Handoffs
use_cases.description: Convert scattered review notes into a consistent artifact for engineering teams.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Blue Book
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a security Blue Book outline for a sensitive application. Ask for missing context before drafting sections.

prompt_templates.title: Organize Review Evidence
prompt_templates.prompt: Organize these security review notes into Blue Book sections: architecture, assets, controls, risks, decisions, and follow-ups.

prompt_templates.title: Identify Missing Coverage
prompt_templates.scenario: Check a draft for review gaps.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Blue Book draft and list missing security evidence, unclear assumptions, and follow-up questions by priority.

prompt_templates.title: Build Executive Review Material
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an executive-ready Blue Book summary from this review. Include risk posture, major decisions, residual risks, and owners.

output_examples.input: A team needs a Blue Book for a payment workflow before launch.
output: A structured outline covering scope, data flows, trust boundaries, controls, risks, and launch decisions.
A short list of missing evidence, such as threat model status and incident response ownership.

output_examples.input: A security lead has scattered review notes for a sensitive admin portal.
output: Grouped sections for architecture, privileged actions, authentication, authorization, logging, and open risks.
A prioritized follow-up list with owners, review questions, and decision points.

output_examples.input: An executive wants a release risk summary from a completed review.
output_examples.output: A concise summary of risk posture, accepted risks, blocking issues, and recommended next steps.

best_practices: Provide architecture diagrams, data classifications, and known control evidence before drafting.
Record assumptions, owners, and decision dates for every major risk.
Separate confirmed findings from unanswered review questions.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the Blue Book as a substitute for technical testing.
Do not omit residual risks or unresolved ownership gaps.
Do not include secrets, credentials, or private keys in prompts.

faq.question: What is a security Blue Book?
faq.answer: It is a structured review artifact for application security context, risks, controls, and decisions.

faq.question: Does this skill run security scans?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and documentation structure only.

faq.question: Can it replace a security engineer?
faq.answer: No. A qualified reviewer must validate evidence, risks, and final decisions.

faq.question: What input should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide application scope, architecture, data sensitivity, controls, review notes, risks, and launch constraints.

faq.question: Is it suitable for regulated applications?
faq.answer: It can help organize review content, but compliance interpretation needs qualified human review.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: No. The skill file only contains guidance text and repository links.
